model_narrative: We are given an observed binary sequence over a horizon of time steps, and we want to reproduce it as closely as possible with a linear recursive sequence. We freely choose the 0/1 values of the first few seed steps, and every later step is then generated from two earlier steps by a fixed recurrence. We also track how many time steps the generated sequence disagrees with the observed one. The objective is to minimize the total number of disagreements between the generated sequence and the observed sequence over the whole horizon.

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the full time horizon, a sequence of integer time steps in chronological order; the first member is the earliest step and each later member follows the one before it

sets.name: f
sets.doc: the first stretch of time steps that act as the free seed of the sequence; these are the initial steps whose values are chosen directly, and the set has exactly as many members as the recurrence order

params.name: c
params.doc: the observed target sequence to be fitted, taking the value 0 or 1 at each time step

params.name: n
params.doc: the recurrence order, equal to the number of seed steps; the recurrence looks back this many steps, a scalar

params.name: r
params.doc: the recurrence shift parameter, a scalar

params.name: n_minus_r
params.doc: the second look-back distance of the recurrence, equal to the recurrence order minus the shift parameter, a scalar

vars.name: k
vars.domain: Binary on the seed steps f, continuous in the unit interval [0,1] on the remaining steps
vars.doc: the fitted recursive sequence value at each time step; on the seed steps it is a 0/1 decision, and on every later step it is determined in the unit interval and takes a 0/1 value automatically once the seed is fixed

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total number of disagreements between the fitted sequence and the observed target sequence over the whole horizon

constraints.name: z
constraints.intent: Count the total number of time steps where the generated sequence and the observed sequence disagree, and set the disagreement total equal to that count. At a step where the observed value is zero, a disagreement happens when the generated value is one, and at a step where the observed value is one, a disagreement happens when the generated value is zero. Add up these mismatches over every step in the horizon.

constraints.name: n),
constraints.intent: For every step that the recurrence generates, the generated value cannot exceed the sum of the two earlier values it is built from, namely the value one recurrence order back and the value the second look-back distance back. This applies only to the steps reached by the recurrence, where both of those earlier steps fall inside the horizon.

constraints.name: n),
constraints.intent: For every step that the recurrence generates, the generated value cannot exceed two minus the two earlier values it is built from, namely the value one recurrence order back and the value the second look-back distance back. This applies only to the steps reached by the recurrence, where both of those earlier steps fall inside the horizon.

constraints.name: n),
constraints.intent: For every step that the recurrence generates, the generated value must be at least the value the second look-back distance back minus the value one recurrence order back. This applies only to the steps reached by the recurrence, where both of those earlier steps fall inside the horizon.

constraints.name: n),
constraints.intent: For every step that the recurrence generates, the generated value must be at least the value one recurrence order back minus the value the second look-back distance back. This applies only to the steps reached by the recurrence, where both of those earlier steps fall inside the horizon.

query: There's a proposal on the table to keep the seed lean: the number of ones across the leading recurrence bits should stay no higher than half the seed length. What disagreement count does the optimizer return once we cap the seed that way?
expected_pyomo: model.sparse_seed = Constraint(expr=sum(model.k[t] for t in model.f) <= 0.5 * len(model.f))

query: We're looking at a front-loading rule for the seed — the ones sitting in the back half of the seed window shouldn't make up more than 35% of all the seed ones taken together. Plug that in and tell me the resulting fit quality.
expected_pyomo: model.tail_share = Constraint(expr=sum(model.k[t] for t in model.f if t > len(model.f)//2) <= 0.35 * sum(model.k[t] for t in model.f))

query: Engineering is pitching a clean-start rule where at most one of the three opening seed bits is allowed to be switched on. What does the optimizer come back with for the disagreement count under that restriction?

query: The board wants two seed rules packaged together: the back half of the seed window should carry at most 30% of all the seed ones, and at most one of the three opening bits should be on. Run both at once and walk me through the resulting fit.
expected_pyomo:
model.tail_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.k[t] for t in model.f if t > len(model.f)//2) <= 0.3 * sum(model.k[t] for t in model.f))
model.lead_exclusive = Constraint(expr=model.k[1] + model.k[2] + model.k[3] <= 1)

query: The fitted seed runs sparser than I'd expect for a signal this active. Shouldn't the seed be carrying ones across at least ninety percent of its length to stay in step with the data? What's keeping the optimizer from running a denser seed than that?
expected_pyomo: model.dense_seed = Constraint(expr=sum(model.k[t] for t in model.f) >= 0.9 * len(model.f))

constraints.name: z
constraints.intent: Count the total number of time steps where the generated sequence and the observed sequence disagree, and set the disagreement total equal to that count. At a step where the observed value is zero, a disagreement happens when the generated value is one, and at a step where the observed value is one, a disagreement happens when the generated value is zero. Add up these mismatches over every step in the horizon.

constraints.name: n),
constraints.intent: For every step that the recurrence generates, the generated value cannot exceed the sum of the two earlier values it is built from, namely the value one recurrence order back and the value the second look-back distance back. This applies only to the steps reached by the recurrence, where both of those earlier steps fall inside the horizon.

constraints.name: n),
constraints.intent: For every step that the recurrence generates, the generated value cannot exceed two minus the two earlier values it is built from, namely the value one recurrence order back and the value the second look-back distance back. This applies only to the steps reached by the recurrence, where both of those earlier steps fall inside the horizon.

constraints.name: n),
constraints.intent: For every step that the recurrence generates, the generated value must be at least the value the second look-back distance back minus the value one recurrence order back. This applies only to the steps reached by the recurrence, where both of those earlier steps fall inside the horizon.

constraints.name: n),
constraints.intent: For every step that the recurrence generates, the generated value must be at least the value one recurrence order back minus the value the second look-back distance back. This applies only to the steps reached by the recurrence, where both of those earlier steps fall inside the horizon.

query: I'd want the seed both lean and clean. Couldn't we just hold the total seed ones to no more than half the seed length while also allowing at most one of the three opening bits to be on? What's the model trading off by refusing to run both of those together?
expected_pyomo:
model.lean_seed = Constraint(expr=sum(model.k[t] for t in model.f) <= 0.5 * len(model.f))
model.lead_exclusive = Constraint(expr=model.k[1] + model.k[2] + model.k[3] <= 1)
